welcomeHint: Mention @OpenClaw or @Hermes to let that AI answer first. Execution still belongs to the current main AI; daily chat can self-correct and acknowledge fixes.
inputPlaceholder: Type a message / Enter to send
qrReady: QR code is ready. Scan it to pair.
pairingReady: Pairing page is ready. Open it to authorize.
qrUnavailable: No valid QR code or pairing link was returned.
pairPending: Pairing has not completed yet. Scan with WeChat, confirm on the phone, then try again.
riskyConfirmPrompt: Risky delete/reset/clear-style action detected. Confirm that the current executor may continue?
riskyConfirmRequired: Risky actions require user confirmation before continuing.
turnEmpty: Select a turn to inspect details
retryPrepared: The turn message is back in the input. Review it, then send.
continuePrepared: The remote message is back in the input. Review it, then send.
ocCliHint: 💡 Leave empty to auto-detect from PATH and common install locations.
ocGatewayHint: 💡 Use ps aux | grep openclaw to inspect the port.
ocModeHint: Use Agent execution mode when OpenClaw is the main AI for code or command tasks. Infer is only suitable for quick chat and planning.
hermesCliHint: 💡 Use which hermes to inspect the path.
wxPluginBundled: Official WeChat plugin is bundled and waiting for install/pairing
wxInstalledNeedsPair: Official WeChat plugin is installed and waiting for QR pairing
wxInstallOk: Official WeChat plugin install/repair finished. Get a QR code next.
wxInstallIncomplete: Official WeChat plugin install/repair did not complete
wxConnectedNoPair: WeChat is connected on the OpenClaw side, but this Gateway is not exposing an openable pairing entry
wxReady: Built-in remote channel is available and waiting for pairing
wechatIntegratedHint: DualMind bundles Tencent's official OpenClaw WeChat plugin and installer. This device keeps one current WeChat account; repeated pairing clears stale records for the same user.
roleHint: This only decides who executes tasks. Mentions change speaking order, not execution ownership; daily chat, correction acknowledgments, and debate stay inside the group flow.
executionCapabilityHint: Execution tasks first check whether the current main AI can really edit files or run commands. If not, DualMind blocks the task instead of pretending it executed.
currentExecReady: The current main AI has execution capability and can handle code or command tasks.
currentExecBlocked: The current main AI does not have real execution capability. Execution tasks will be blocked until you switch the main AI or update adapter configuration.
settingConfirmRisky: Require user confirmation before risky actions
allowRemoteCode: Allow WeChat remote messages to trigger code execution
allowRemoteCodeHint: Keep this off for public use. When off, WeChat and other remote messages enter turn records for the user to continue in the Web UI; when on, remote coding/risky messages may enter the execution flow.
docsOverviewBody: DualMind is a local dual-AI group collaboration interface that puts the user, OpenClaw, and Hermes into one shared chat. Both AIs may speak in daily conversation; the current main AI is always the standing execution holder, and real file, command, or configuration actions are handled only by that main AI while the supporting AI communicates, calibrates, debates, reviews, and flags risk.
docsQuickStartSteps:
• Use Settings to verify that OpenClaw and Hermes are available.
  • Choose one of the two role modes: OpenClaw main or Hermes main.
  • Send messages in the group chat; without a mention, both AIs collaborate under the current main/supporting relationship.
  • Use @OpenClaw or @Hermes only to change who answers first. It does not change the current executor.
  • For code, command, file, or configuration changes, DualMind creates an Action Lease and allows only the current main AI to act.
docsSafetyBody: DualMind separates conversation from execution by default: chat can be free, but execution is locked. The supporting AI may disagree, correct, suggest, or veto risky work, but it must not claim to edit files, run commands, or wait for execution permission to write or save later. Risky actions require user confirmation; WeChat and other remote messages enter turn records by default and cannot directly trigger code execution.
docsStreamingBody: A thinking animation is shown before AI replies. The current OpenClaw/Hermes CLI adapters use simulated streaming: the server receives the full reply, then displays it in chunks. Every turn is saved as a structured record with participants, executor, Action Lease, status, messages, and events.
docsWechatBody: DualMind includes Tencent's official OpenClaw WeChat plugin and unified installer as project dependencies. Settings can install/repair the plugin and generate the official scan QR code. This device keeps one current WeChat account; direct automatic OpenClaw/Hermes replies in WeChat require a later DualMind bridge layer.
docsWechatSteps:
• Open Settings and enable WeChat
  • Click “Install/repair official WeChat plugin”
  • Click “📱 Get QR code”
  • Scan with WeChat on your phone and confirm authorization
  • Click “✅ Scanned, start pairing” to check this login
  • Verify the OpenClaw Weixin channel is running; DualMind webhook remote messages still continue safely in the Web UI
docsRoleTable: ModeDescriptionOpenClaw main / Hermes supportingBoth AIs may speak in daily chat. Mentioning Hermes can make Hermes answer first, but execution tasks still give OpenClaw the Action Lease while Hermes communicates, calibrates, debates, and reviews.Hermes main / OpenClaw supportingBoth AIs may speak in daily chat. Mentioning OpenClaw can make OpenClaw answer first, but execution tasks still give Hermes the Action Lease while OpenClaw communicates, calibrates, debates, and reviews.
docsCalibrationBody: If one AI gives a clearly wrong factual, time, role, or safety answer, the other AI may correct it. The corrected AI briefly acknowledges the fix and closes the loop. If nothing is wrong, DualMind does not add extra noise.
faqOpenClawA: Check whether the Gateway is running. Try curl localhost:18789/health in a terminal.
faqHermesA: Confirm Hermes is installed and available: hermes --version
faqWechatQ: Q: WeChat messages do not arrive?
faqWechatA: Confirm the WeChat remote channel is enabled, then install/repair Tencent's official OpenClaw WeChat plugin in Settings. Getting a QR code must show a real scan QR; if it opens the OpenClaw control page, no WeChat QR was returned.
faqPortQ: Q: How do I change the port?
docsDonationBody: If DualMind is useful to you, you can support continued maintenance with the WeChat payment QR code.
docsFeedbackBody: If you have ideas, suggestions, or feedback while using this program, email the author at kkkliao@88.com.
setupWelcome: DualMind puts the user, OpenClaw, and Hermes into one local shared group chat. Both AIs can speak naturally in daily conversation. For code and command execution, role modes and Action Leases decide who may act.
setupDetectHint: If detection fails, you can fill paths manually later in Settings.
setupOcModeHint: Choose Agent execution mode if OpenClaw should really handle code or command tasks as the main AI. Infer is for quick chat and planning.
setupRoleHint: Main/supporting roles only apply to code, command, and configuration changes. Mentions only change who speaks first; daily chat can naturally self-correct and acknowledge fixes.
setupLocalOnly: Bind to local machine by default
setupConfirmRisky: Require user confirmation before risky actions
setupRemoteCode: Allow remote messages to trigger code execution
setupSafetyHint: Keeping the default safety settings is recommended. Remote messages should not directly trigger code edits.
